title: Phanera longiseta
intro: Phanera longiseta, also known as the long-branched Phanera, is a species of plant in the family Caesalpiniaceae, native to India and Southeast Asia. The health benefits of Phanera longiseta include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties, which make it useful in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Phanera longiseta include decoctions, infusions, and powder, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, particularly those related to the respiratory and digestive systems.
taxonomy: Phanera longiseta is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. It is native to Southeast Asia. The classification of Phanera longiseta places it within the genus Phanera. The genus Phanera is part of the subfamily Phaneroideae. The subfamily Phaneroideae is a member of the family Euphorbiaceae.
uses: Phanera longiseta is a plant species, however, detailed information on its medicinal uses is not readily available.
